meaning: vt.使平均,使平衡,弥补n.平衡量,平衡力,势均力敌
examples: Perhaps you have awful faults to counterbalance your few good points.
The accused's right to silence was a vital counterbalance to the powers of the police.
Advertisers sometimes offset or counterbalance an exaggerated claim with a disclaimer—a qualification or condition on the claim.
Instead, opponents argued, government management was required to counterbalance the power of large corporations.
These opposing attempts to go at a 45-degree angle counterbalance into a 90-degree movement.
